---
title: "Salary & Compensation Trends"
author: "Nutchanon Chaiyaratana"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cerulean
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    embed-resources: true
date-modified: today
date-format: long
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: true
  freeze: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---
## Salary & Compensation Trends

This section analyzes salary and compensation patterns in 2024 job postings using the Lightcast dataset. The goal is to understand how salaries vary across occupations, industries, locations, employment types, and remote work arrangements.

Because salary information is not available for every job posting, this analysis focuses on records with valid salary values greater than zero.

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| output: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, monotonically_increasing_id

spark = SparkSession.builder \
    .config("spark.driver.host", "localhost") \
    .appName("SalaryCompensationTrends") \
    .getOrCreate()

spark.catalog.clearCache()

df = spark.read.option("header", "true") \
    .option("inferSchema", "true") \
    .option("multiLine", "true") \
    .option("escape", "\"") \
    .csv("data/lightcast_job_postings.csv")

df.createOrReplaceTempView("raw_jobs")
# Basic cleaning: remove records without a job posting ID

df_clean = df.filter(col("ID").isNotNull())

df_clean.createOrReplaceTempView("raw_jobs")

print("Original rows:", df.count())

print("Rows after removing missing ID:", df_clean.count())

# companies table
companies_df = df_clean.select(
    col("COMPANY").alias("company_id"),
    col("COMPANY").alias("company"),
    col("COMPANY_NAME").alias("company_name"),
    col("COMPANY_RAW").alias("company_raw"),
    col("COMPANY_IS_STAFFING").alias("company_is_staffing")
).dropDuplicates(["company_id"])

companies_df.createOrReplaceTempView("companies")

# industries table
industries_df = df_clean.select(
    col("NAICS_2022_6").alias("naics_2022_6"),
    col("NAICS_2022_6_NAME").alias("naics_2022_6_name")
).dropDuplicates()

industries_df = industries_df.withColumn(
    "industry_id",
    monotonically_increasing_id()
)

industries_df = industries_df.select(
    "industry_id",
    "naics_2022_6",
    "naics_2022_6_name"
)

industries_df.createOrReplaceTempView("industries")

# locations table
locations_df = df_clean.select(
    col("LOCATION").alias("location"),
    col("CITY_NAME").alias("city_name"),
    col("STATE_NAME").alias("state_name"),
    col("COUNTY_NAME").alias("county_name"),
    col("MSA").alias("msa"),
    col("MSA_NAME").alias("msa_name")
).dropDuplicates()

locations_df = locations_df.withColumn(
    "location_id",
    monotonically_increasing_id()
)

locations_df = locations_df.select(
    "location_id",
    "location",
    "city_name",
    "state_name",
    "county_name",
    "msa",
    "msa_name"
)

locations_df.createOrReplaceTempView("locations")

# occupations table
occupations_df = df_clean.select(
    col("SOC_5").alias("soc_5"),
    col("SOC_5_NAME").alias("soc_5_name"),
    col("LOT_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION").alias("specialized_occupation"),
    col("LOT_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION_NAME").alias("specialized_occupation_name"),
    col("LOT_OCCUPATION_GROUP").alias("occupation_group"),
    col("LOT_OCCUPATION_GROUP_NAME").alias("occupation_group_name")
).dropDuplicates()

occupations_df = occupations_df.withColumn(
    "occupation_id",
    monotonically_increasing_id()
)

occupations_df = occupations_df.select(
    "occupation_id",
    "soc_5",
    "soc_5_name",
    "specialized_occupation",
    "specialized_occupation_name",
    "occupation_group",
    "occupation_group_name"
)

occupations_df.createOrReplaceTempView("occupations")

# job postings table
job_with_industry = df_clean.join(
    industries_df,
    (
        (df["NAICS_2022_6"] == industries_df["naics_2022_6"]) &
        (df["NAICS_2022_6_NAME"] == industries_df["naics_2022_6_name"])
    ),
    "left"
)

job_with_location = job_with_industry.join(
    locations_df,
    (
        (job_with_industry["LOCATION"] == locations_df["location"]) &
        (job_with_industry["CITY_NAME"] == locations_df["city_name"]) &
        (job_with_industry["STATE_NAME"] == locations_df["state_name"]) &
        (job_with_industry["COUNTY_NAME"] == locations_df["county_name"]) &
        (job_with_industry["MSA"] == locations_df["msa"]) &
        (job_with_industry["MSA_NAME"] == locations_df["msa_name"])
    ),
    "left"
)

job_with_keys = job_with_location.join(
    occupations_df,
    (
        (job_with_location["SOC_5"] == occupations_df["soc_5"]) &
        (job_with_location["SOC_5_NAME"] == occupations_df["soc_5_name"]) &
        (job_with_location["LOT_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION"] == occupations_df["specialized_occupation"]) &
        (job_with_location["LOT_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION_NAME"] == occupations_df["specialized_occupation_name"]) &
        (job_with_location["LOT_OCCUPATION_GROUP"] == occupations_df["occupation_group"]) &
        (job_with_location["LOT_OCCUPATION_GROUP_NAME"] == occupations_df["occupation_group_name"])
    ),
    "left"
)

job_postings_df = job_with_keys.select(
    job_with_keys["ID"].alias("id"),
    job_with_keys["TITLE_CLEAN"].alias("title_clean"),
    job_with_keys["COMPANY"].alias("company_id"),
    col("industry_id"),
    col("occupation_id"),
    job_with_keys["EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME"].alias("employment_type_name"),
    job_with_keys["REMOTE_TYPE_NAME"].alias("remote_type_name"),
    job_with_keys["BODY"].alias("body"),
    job_with_keys["MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE"].alias("min_years_experience"),
    job_with_keys["MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE"].alias("max_years_experience"),
    job_with_keys["SALARY"].alias("salary"),
    job_with_keys["SALARY_FROM"].alias("salary_from"),
    job_with_keys["SALARY_TO"].alias("salary_to"),
    col("location_id"),
    job_with_keys["POSTED"].alias("posted"),
    job_with_keys["EXPIRED"].alias("expired"),
    job_with_keys["DURATION"].alias("duration")
).filter(
    col("id").isNotNull()
).dropDuplicates(["id"])

job_postings_df.createOrReplaceTempView("job_postings")

job_postings_df.createOrReplaceTempView("job_postings")
```
### Validate Registered SQL Tables
Before running the salary analysis queries, I validate that all relational tables were created and registered in Spark SQL.

```{python}

def style_table(df, format_dict=None):

    styled = df.style

    if format_dict is not None:

        styled = styled.format(format_dict)

    styled = styled.set_properties(**{

        "text-align": "left",

        "white-space": "normal",

        "border": "1px solid black"

    }).set_table_styles([

        {

            "selector": "th",

            "props": [

                ("border", "1px solid black"),

                ("text-align", "left"),

                ("background-color", "#f2f2f2")

            ]

        },

        {

            "selector": "td",

            "props": [

                ("border", "1px solid black"),

                ("padding", "6px")

            ]

        },

        {

            "selector": "table",

            "props": [

                ("border-collapse", "collapse")

            ]

        }

    ])

    return styled

```
```{python}
table_counts = spark.sql("""
SELECT 'job_postings' AS table_name, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM job_postings
UNION ALL
SELECT 'companies' AS table_name, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM companies
UNION ALL
SELECT 'industries' AS table_name, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM industries
UNION ALL
SELECT 'locations' AS table_name, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM locations
UNION ALL
SELECT 'occupations' AS table_name, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM occupations
""")

table_counts_pd = table_counts.toPandas()

style_table(table_counts_pd, {
    "row_count": "{:,.0f}"
})
```

### Preview Companies Table

```{python}
companies_preview = companies_df.select(
    "company_id",
    "company",
    "company_name",
    "company_raw",
    "company_is_staffing"
).limit(5).toPandas()

style_table(companies_preview)
```

### Preview Industries Table

```{python}
industries_preview = industries_df.select(
    "industry_id",
    "naics_2022_6",
    "naics_2022_6_name"
).limit(5).toPandas()

style_table(industries_preview)
```

### Preview Locations Table

```{python}
locations_preview = locations_df.select(
    "location_id",
    "city_name",
    "state_name",
    "county_name",
    "msa",
    "msa_name"
).limit(5).toPandas()

style_table(locations_preview)
```

### Preview Occupations Table

```{python}
occupations_preview = occupations_df.select(
    "occupation_id",
    "soc_5",
    "soc_5_name",
    "specialized_occupation",
    "specialized_occupation_name",
    "occupation_group",
    "occupation_group_name"
).limit(5).toPandas()

style_table(occupations_preview)
```

### Preview Job Postings Table

```{python}
job_postings_preview = job_postings_df.select(
    "id",
    "title_clean",
    "company_id",
    "industry_id",
    "occupation_id",
    "location_id",
    "salary",
    "posted"
).limit(5).toPandas()

style_table(job_postings_preview)
```
